assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.label: Conceptual and mental-model review
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic is primarily definitions, mental models, comparisons, or framework purpose. Scenario MCQs are more appropriate than forced code trivia.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.

commonMistakes: Marking a large route tree as client code when only a small interaction needs it.
Confusing React Server Components with server-side rendering.
Assuming cached data is fresh without an explicit policy and invalidation path.
Placing security-sensitive validation only in the client or middleware layer.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.
contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.

learningObjectives.text: Compare client-side versus server-side rendering using a new example and justify the result.
Trace and explain ssr request flow using a new example and justify the result.
Explain hydration and interactivity using a new example and justify the result.

mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Client-side versus server-side rendering** → **SSR request flow** → **Hydration and interactivity**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Server-Side Rendering and Hydration rule and the observable result.
choices.label: Hydration connects React behavior to matching server-rendered HTML. The first client render must agree with server output.

sections.body: Client-side rendering creates much of the UI in the browser; server-side rendering creates HTML on a server or during prerendering. Modern applications often combine both.
sections.title: Client-side versus server-side rendering

sections.body: The server receives a request, resolves data, renders HTML, and returns it so content can appear before all client JavaScript is ready.
sections.title: SSR request flow

sections.body: Hydration connects React behavior to matching server-rendered HTML. The first client render must agree with server output.
sections.title: Hydration and interactivity

sections.explanation: HTML appears first and hydration attaches React behavior to the matching tree.
sections.title: Worked example

summary: Server-Side Rendering and Hydration connects Client-side versus server-side rendering, SSR request flow, Hydration and interactivity.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.
title: Server-Side Rendering and Hydration

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

whyThisMatters: Server-Side Rendering and Hydration is part of the mental model learners need before making reliable implementation decisions. It belongs to the **Rendering Foundations** module.
